title: pavonia formosa
intro: Pavonia formosa, also known as Rose Mallow or Wild Mallow, is a perennial herb native to the Americas, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Pavonia formosa have been found to help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and support immune function.
